name: Pier Code

form_factor.comment: Terminal-native coding agent installed as a standalone `pier` binary for macOS, Linux, and Windows. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://www.piercode.com/.

released_in.comment: Pier changelog lists v0.1.0 stable on June 17, 2026 as the first public release.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest public release v0.4.1 (July 7, 2026) saves plan-mode output to a file while the agent writes it and lets approved plans jump straight into Auto mode.

pricing.value: Pro $5/mo; Max $20/mo; $5 usage top-ups; Max enables frontier models including OpenAI, Anthropic, Kimi and more
pricing.comment: Pricing page lists Pro at $5/month, Max at $20/month with 4x credits and frontier providers, plus $5 usage packs.

some_free_usage.comment: No documented free tier found; current plans are paid early-access plans.

no_account_required.comment: Pier is local-first and has OSS/local-provider flags, but normal product use is tied to Pier plan/login access.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Max plan and CLI model/provider flags support frontier providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Kimi, and local/open-source provider modes.

rules.comment: CLI help exposes `--ignore-rules`, confirming user/project execpolicy rule files are normally loaded.

skills.comment: Plugin support is first-class, but public docs reviewed here do not describe a SKILL.md-style skill folder.

hooks.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

mcp_servers.comment: Local CLI help verifies `pier mcp`, `pier mcp-server`, and MCP management commands.

custom_commands.comment: Product page and CLI help show built-in slash workflows such as `/review`; no user-defined slash-command system was confirmed.

goal_command.comment: No `/goal` command or persistent goal objective was found in current public docs/help.

subagents.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

monitor.comment: v0.4.0 adds listing/stopping background terminals, but no full Monitor-equivalent background watch primitive was confirmed.

transcripts.comment: Local CLI help verifies persisted sessions plus `resume`, `archive`, `delete`, `unarchive`, `fork`, and non-interactive `--ephemeral`.

model_selection.comment: Supports `--model`; public pricing lists Sarvam 105B/30B/Pier Hybrid plus Max frontier models.

approval_mode.comment: CLI help verifies `--ask-for-approval` values untrusted, on-request, on-failure, and never.

sandbox_mode.comment: CLI help verifies sandbox modes read-only, workspace-write, and danger-full-access plus bypass flag.

resume.comment: CLI help verifies `pier resume` and `pier exec resume` with `--last`.

continue.comment: `pier resume --last` continues the most recent session.

non_interactive.comment: Local CLI help verifies `pier exec` and `pier review` non-interactive commands.

output_format.comment: `pier exec --json` prints JSONL events and `--output-last-message` writes the final message to a file.

statusline.comment: Terminal UI shows model/mode/cwd/status, but no custom statusline API was confirmed.

telemetry.comment: Homepage says no telemetry you did not ask for; no detailed opt-out settings were found.

custom_model_provider.comment: CLI help verifies `--oss` and `--local-provider` for lmstudio/ollama plus explicit `--model`.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Product screenshots document terminal shortcuts such as Alt+T to hide tasks.

theme_customization.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

auto_save.comment: v0.4.1 saves plan-mode output to a file as the agent writes it.

debugging_output.comment: CLI help verifies `doctor`, `debug`, `features`, and JSONL event output.

context_window.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

code_formatting.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

git_integration.comment: Product page emphasizes diffed/gated changes; CLI help includes `apply` for latest agent diff and `review`.

performance_mode.comment: Model choices include Pier Hybrid and Sarvam 30B/105B, but no named performance mode beyond model/mode selection was confirmed.

ui_customization.comment: Terminal UI includes toggles such as hiding tasks, but broader UI customization was not confirmed.

hosted_agent.comment: v0.3.2 introduced remote execution in the cloud, but the primary CLI remains local and remote execution is presented as a feature rather than a separate managed platform.

notes: Pier Code is a terminal-native paid coding agent by PierCode, installed from dl.piercode.com as `pier`. Local help verified version 0.4.1 with exec/review/resume/fork/cloud/remote-control/MCP/plugin/sandbox/approval/model/JSONL surfaces.
